title: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) — range-Doppler coherent imaging
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical remote-sensing literature for this principle.

E.description: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) — range-Doppler coherent imaging: sar coherent imaging produces the measurement through a 5-node primitive DAG L.emit.chirp -> S.scan.platform -> L.pulse_compression -> L.range_doppler -> int.spatial, with multi-angle tomographic integration and multiplicative speckle (Rayleigh amplitude / exponential intensity). Recovery is posed as a non-convex inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 2D complex scattering. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~20; calibration-level mismatch (platform_motion_error, ionospheric_phase, speckle_coherence_loss) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: y(t, phi) = chirp conv s(r, a) * exp(i*4*pi*R/lambda) + n; recover complex scattering field s
E.world_state_x: Scene-side quantity of interest (reflectance / velocity / phase / concentration)
E.observation_y: Sensor output at declared resolution + noise

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D complex scattering is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness is local rather than global (non-convex landscape); convergence depends on initialisation and priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 20); platform_motion_error dominates the stability cliff; ionospheric_phase and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Multiplicative speckle (rayleigh amplitude / exponential intensity) sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while TV / wavelet-sparsity / deep priors stabilise recovery at the ill-conditioned end of Omega.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [Range-Doppler, Omega-K, CS-SAR] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [SAR-Net]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_F / ||x||_F <= C1 / sqrt(N_independent_looks). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |platform_motion_error| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
